original_hypothesis.title: LendingTerm.forgive() On Loan In Active Auction Permanently Corrupts AuctionHouse State
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: LendingTerm.forgive() does not check if the loan has been called and is in an active auction (callTime != 0). If governor calls forgive() on an auctioned loan, it sets closeTime and decrements issuance. Subsequently, AuctionHouse.bid() and AuctionHouse.forgive() both call LendingTerm.onBid() which requires closeTime == 0, causing a permanent revert. The auction becomes unsettleable, nAuctionsInProgress is permanently elevated, and setAuctionHouse (which requires nAuctionsInProgress == 0) is permanently blocked.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The loan lifecycle state machine allows an illegal transition from Called to Closed via forgive(), bypassing the auction settlement path, creating an orphaned auction.

proof_of_signal.pattern: forgive() checks borrowTime != 0 and closeTime == 0 but NOT callTime == 0; onBid requires closeTime == 0

preconditions: Governor (GOVERNOR role) calls forgive on a loan that has been called
An auction exists for the loan in AuctionHouse

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: forgive() only checks borrowTime and closeTime. If callTime > 0, the auction in AuctionHouse becomes orphaned. nAuctionsInProgress never decrements.
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No check for callTime == 0 in forgive(); no mechanism to settle orphaned auctions

analysis_questions: Does nAuctionsInProgress being permanently > 0 block other critical operations?
Can emergencyAction fix the AuctionHouse state?
Is issuance double-decremented if both forgive and onBid succeed somehow?
validation_requirements: Trace forgive() on called loan -> verify auction stuck

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Governor calls forgive(loanId) on a loan currently in auction (e.g., emergency with frozen collateral). Loan is closed but auction remains active and unsettleable. nAuctionsInProgress is permanently stuck at >= 1. setAuctionHouse can never be called again.
prerequisites: Governor action on an actively auctioned loan

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: AuctionHouse migration permanently blocked; potential protocol upgrade path broken
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: Protocol governance and future auction system upgrades
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Only through governor emergencyAction to directly modify AuctionHouse storage

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing test for forgive on called loan

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
